title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 10 - 2017: Collecting all Greco-Roman books

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a brief vlog update about his ongoing project of digitally collecting all surviving Greco-Roman texts. He reflects on the scope of the work—particularly Plato and Aristotle—and shares his goal of calculating how much of human recorded knowledge could theoretically be memorized in a lifetime, comparing brain storage to computer storage.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Enthusiastic and committed; feels he can cover all surviving works including the big ones like Plato and Aristotle
Believes he gains substantial knowledge just from cataloging books—structure, size, scope
Speculates that reading all day every day for a lifetime might fill only about one fifth of the brain's total storage capacity
Interested in estimating how many pages were lost from antiquity based on known gaps in the surviving record

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle didn't get the time to do a video yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been focusing on books related to the Greco-Roman world for the past couple of days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle decided to collect everything from the classical world up until the fall of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels exhausted from collecting material on Rome and is now focusing on Greece.

claims.subject: Plato and Aristotle
claims.claim: Plato and Aristotle wrote a lot, and collecting their works would take the most time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels like he is learning a lot just by collecting the books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is excited to finish the books and do a word count to understand their size and structure.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been working into the morning and sleeping a little but getting a lot done.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been listening to books while working.

claims.subject: Plato's Republic
claims.claim: Kyle was listening to Plato's Republic and plans to work on it next.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has been bouncing around exploring different materials and feels he can zoom through them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's goal is to memorize all the information and set it up on his website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to calculate how much information the human brain can hold in a lifetime from reading.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to compare the storage capacity of the brain to that of a computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle estimates that reading all day every day might use up one-fifth of the brain's total storage capacity.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is currently working on detailing mood and then plans to focus on Plato and Aristotle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels that after conquering Plato and Aristotle, he will have the rest of the Greco-Roman material.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is looking forward to condensing all the Greco-Roman world's information onto one page.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to estimate how many pages of lost books there are based on known information.
